prompt_text: Create a healthy weekly meal plan.
response_text:
Here is a healthy, balanced weekly meal plan designed to be flexible, nutrient-dense, and easy to follow. It focuses on whole foods: lean proteins, healthy fats, complex carbohydrates, and plenty of vegetables.

**Key Principles:**
- **Hydration:** Drink water, herbal tea, or infused water throughout the day.
- **Portion Control:** Fill half your plate with vegetables, a quarter with lean protein, and a quarter with whole grains or starchy vegetables.
- **Prep Ahead:** Wash and chop veggies, cook a batch of quinoa or brown rice, and hard-boil eggs on Sunday.

---

### Weekly Meal Plan

| Day       | Breakfast                                    | Lunch                                            | Dinner                                            | Snack (Optional)                     |
| :-------- | :------------------------------------------- | :----------------------------------------------- | :------------------------------------------------ | :----------------------------------- |
| **Monday**  | Greek yogurt with berries, a sprinkle of chia seeds & almonds | Large salad with mixed greens, grilled chicken, chickpeas, cucumber, bell peppers, and lemon-tahini dressing | Baked salmon with roasted broccoli and sweet potato wedges | Apple slices with 1 tbsp peanut butter |
| **Tuesday** | Oatmeal made with oats, milk (dairy or plant), topped with sliced banana & walnuts | Leftover salmon and roasted vegetables over a bed of quinoa | Turkey & black bean chili (canned or homemade) with a side salad | A small handful of almonds           |
| **Wednesday**| 2 scrambled eggs with spinach and 1 slice of whole-grain toast | Leftover turkey chili with a dollop of Greek yogurt and sliced avocado | Stir-fry: shrimp, broccoli, carrots, snap peas, and bell peppers over brown rice | Carrot & celery sticks with hummus   |
| **Thursday**| Smoothie: spinach, frozen mango, plain kefir, 1 tbsp flaxseed, and a splash of water | Large salad with lentils, chopped veggies, feta cheese, and a simple vinaigrette | Lean pork chop with sautéed kale and roasted butternut squash | A hard-boiled egg                    |
| **Friday**  | Cottage cheese with sliced peaches and a few chopped pecans | Leftover pork chop, kale, and squash (reheat and enjoy) | Whole-wheat pasta with lean ground turkey, tomato sauce (low-sugar), and a side of steamed green beans | A small pear                         |
| **Saturday**| 2 whole-grain pancakes (made with minimal added sugar) with fresh berries and a dollop of Greek yogurt | Large veggie & hummus wrap: whole-wheat tortilla, hummus, mixed greens, shredded carrots, bell peppers, and cucumber | Lean sirloin steak (grilled or pan-seared) with roasted asparagus and a baked potato (with skin) | Handful of grapes                    |
| **Sunday**  | Veggie omelet (mushrooms, onions, bell peppers) with 1 slice of whole-grain toast | "Buddha bowl": quinoa, roasted chickpeas, avocado, shredded red cabbage, and a drizzle of tahini dressing | Roasted chicken breast with a giant side of roasted Brussels sprouts and carrots | 1 oz dark chocolate (70%+ cacao)     |

---

### Grocery Shopping List

**Produce:**
- Berries (fresh or frozen)
- Bananas
- Apples
- Pears
- Peaches (canned in juice or fresh)
- Grapes
- Lemons
- Avocados
- Spinach (fresh)
- Mixed salad greens
- Kale
- Broccoli
- Sweet potatoes
- Carrots
- Celery
- Cucumber
- Bell peppers (various colors)
- Snap peas
- Onions
- Garlic
- Mushrooms
- Butternut squash
- Asparagus
- Brussels sprouts
- Green beans
- Fresh herbs (optional: parsley, cilantro)

**Protein:**
- Chicken breast (boneless, skinless)
- Lean ground turkey (93/7 or 99/1)
- Salmon fillets
- Shrimp (raw, peeled, deveined)
- Lean pork chops
- Lean sirloin steak
- Whole chicken (for roasting)
- Eggs
- Greek yogurt (plain, unsweetened)
- Cottage cheese
- Kefir (plain)

**Pantry & Grains:**
- Rolled oats
- Quinoa
- Brown rice
- Whole-wheat pasta
- Whole-grain bread or tortillas
- Canned chickpeas (low-sodium)
- Canned black beans (low-sodium)
- Canned lentils (low-sodium)
- Canned diced tomatoes (no salt added)
- Tomato sauce (low-sugar)
- Chili seasoning (or cumin, chili powder, paprika)
- Tahini
- Hummus
- Almonds (raw, unsalted)
- Walnuts
- Pecans
- Chia seeds
- Flaxseed (ground)
- Peanut butter (natural, no added sugar)
- Olive oil
- Vinegar (balsamic, apple cider)
- Salt, pepper, your favorite herbs & spices
- Dark chocolate (70%+ cacao)

**Dairy/Alternatives (Optional):**
- Milk (dairy or unsweetened plant-based)
- Feta cheese (optional)

### Tips for Success:
1.  **Double Dinner:** When you cook dinner (e.g., salmon, chili, pork chops), intentionally make extra for lunch the next day.
2.  **Batch Cook:** On Sunday, cook a large batch of quinoa or brown rice, roast a tray of mixed vegetables (broccoli, sweet potatoes, carrots), and hard-boil 4-6 eggs.
3.  **Listen to Your Body:** Adjust portion sizes based on your hunger, activity level, and goals. This is a template, not a strict rule.
4.  **Stay Flexible:** Swap days if needed. Use leftovers creatively (e.g., roasted chicken can become salad or a wrap).

Enjoy your healthy week of eating
